title: Continuous Gaussian-mixture location regression replaces the shared map-cell classification field
hypothesis: 16 straight rounds since champion exp-35 (743.07 m) have failed, and every one of them left one thing structurally intact even when it looked abandoned: SOME shared, jointly-normalized comparison across many location candidates decides the answer -- a flat 1024-way softmax (36-40, 45, 46), a per-region softmax repeated 64 times (50), an attention-weighted mean over 256 votes (47-49), or a hard-argmin residual-VQ codebook lookup (51, which explicitly abandoned softmax for exclusive nearest-neighbor coding and still scored 2751.73 m, 3.7x worse than champion). champion-failure-mode-probes diagnosed the wall precisely: the true cell is the classifier's top-1 pick only ~2% of the time despite landing in its own top-20 (of 1024) on 33-58% of crops -- confusable lookalikes stay comparable under one shared scale no matter how that scale is reparameterized, factorized, shortlisted, or replaced with hard coding. This design tests the one parameterization family absent from all 51 rounds: a small, fully continuous, per-crop Gaussian mixture over (u,v), with each component's mean a FREE regression output (not a fixed grid center, not a codebook entry, not a spatially-partitioned expert), trained by exact mixture negative-log-likelihood (proper probabilistic multimodal regression, Bishop-style MDN) instead of cross-entropy-over-candidates, hard nearest-neighbor coding, or attention-averaged consensus. A K=6-component mixture per rotation view, pooled across the 4 C4 views into one 24-component mixture, gives the model just enough hypotheses to represent genuine ambiguity between a handful of lookalike districts -- without ever forcing 1024, 256, or 2304 candidates into one comparison at once, and without exp 37's winner-take-all-L2 failure mode (dead/uninitialized hypotheses starved of any gradient): every component gets a soft NLL-weighted gradient in proportion to its own likelihood under the true target, every step. The mnv3s_features8.pt / mobilenet_v3_small backbone is banned this round, which forces the trunk to become from-scratch anyway -- rather than treat that as a tax paid while leaving the decision mechanism alone (the exact failure the patience rule calls out), the forced trunk change is the occasion to also delete the GRID_K cell abstraction entirely: with no classification grid anywhere in the model, the champion's training-data mechanism (exp 35's confusability-weighted sampling, computed over 32x32 GRID_K classifier cells) has no cells left to attach to, so it is rebuilt at the level the new architecture actually operates on: individual training locations. This is a genuine whole-pipeline pivot (trunk, decode, loss, confidence, and the sampler all change together because the grid they shared is gone), not a decode swap bolted onto an unexamined trunk.
method: Replace TinyLocNet's ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small trunk + GAP/layout head + gated dark-expert + GRID_K x GRID_K classification field + beta-sharpened soft-argmax decode + field-shape confidence, in model/model.py, with: (1) a from-scratch conv trunk (~4 stride-2 blocks, 128x128 -> 8x8, ~300-400k params, no pretrained weights, no torchvision import); (2) a shared small head that reads {GAP descriptor, 1x1-squeezed spatial layout code} and outputs, per rotation view, K=6 mixture components (mean_u, mean_v via sigmoid to stay in [0,1], a scalar log_sigma, and a mixing logit); (3) the 4 C4 rotation views' components are pooled into one 24-component mixture (concatenate, renormalize mixing weights by softmax jointly over all 24 logits) instead of averaging field logits; (4) decode = the mean of the single highest-weight component (hard mode-commit, matching the project's established peak-commit lesson from exp 14 rather than a soft blended mean, which would reintroduce centroid shrinkage); (5) loss_fn becomes exact 2D-Gaussian mixture NLL over the pooled 24 components (isotropic covariance per component, log_sigma floored for numerical stability) plus the existing conf BCE term, recomputed on new features; (6) confidence is now {winning component's own mixing weight (post-hoc renormalized among the 24), winning component's own sigma, and the mixture weight distribution's entropy over all 24 components} in place of the deleted field's peak-mass/entropy/sharpened-vs-unsharpened-gap features -- same conf_head MLP shape and the same conf_shift calibration mechanism in train.py's calibrate_conf_shift (untouched procedure, only its downstream inputs change since there is no more field to read). In model/train.py: compute_cell_weights' GRID_K x GRID_K grid-cell confusability weighting is replaced by a per-crop-location confusability weight computed directly at the enumerable crop resolution (same cheap RGB+luminance-std descriptor from reference.tif, same nearest-DISTANT-match logic and neighbor exclusion radius, just indexed by individual crop position instead of by a now-nonexistent classification cell) -- crop_probs in train_area is built the same way structurally, just without the crop_cell/GRID_K indirection. The optimizer's param groups collapse from {trunk: 1e-4, head: 1e-3} to a single uniform LR group with cosine annealing (the 10x trunk/head LR split existed specifically to protect pretrained ImageNet features during fine-tuning; a from-scratch trunk has nothing to protect and should train at the same rate as the rest of the network). Epoch count, TRAIN_REALIZATIONS, batch size, and the C4 exact-rotation pixel-copy mechanism (_c4_stack) are unchanged. train.py's CLI (--area/--out-dir/--data-dir/--epochs/--max-crops-per-bucket/--seed) and model/model.py's ONNX export contract (1x3x128x128 float32 in [0,1] -> [[u, v, conf]]) are preserved exactly -- the frozen scorer depends on both. No reference to mnv3s_features8.pt, mobilenet_v3_small, or torchvision.models anywhere in model/ (backbonecheck.py scans the actual source, not self-reported intent).
expected_outcome: If the true wall really is shared-scale candidate comparability (the theory all 16 reverted rounds jointly point at), worst-case median error improves from champion's 743.07 m to roughly 550-850 m (7-26%), because no two components anywhere in the decode are ever forced into a >24-way joint comparison, and every component gets a genuine soft-likelihood gradient every step (unlike exp 37's winner-take-all regression, which had no such pressure and collapsed toward the map centroid). But exp 51's residual-VQ result (2751.73 m, far worse than champion, despite also abandoning shared-softmax comparability via exclusive hard coding) is a real prior data point against a clean win here too, and this experiment's own falsification value is highest either way. Mechanism-level signature to check post-hoc: per-crop responsibility (posterior weight given the true target) should be spread across at least 2-4 of the 24 components on genuinely ambiguous crops, not collapsed onto one dead component for the whole area (classic MDN mode collapse); winning-component sigma should correlate negatively with actual error (smaller predicted sigma on crops the model gets right). Falsification forks: (a) if the metric improves and responsibility/sigma calibration both look healthy, comparability-under-a-shared-scale is confirmed as the real wall and future rounds should keep exploring this family (different K, learned full covariance, deeper trunk); (b) if training is healthy (stable NLL descent, no component collapse, reasonable sigma spread) but the primary metric does not clear 743.07 m, that is a materially new and strong result after 16 rounds: it would mean five structurally distinct decision mechanisms -- classification softmax, attention-weighted consensus, spatial mixture-of-experts, hard VQ coding, and now continuous mixture-density regression -- have all failed to beat the confusability-sampling champion, redirecting future rounds decisively away from decode/decision-mechanism churn and toward raw trunk/descriptor information content (the from-scratch capacity itself, or a permissively-licensed non-mobilenet pretrained family) as the real bottleneck; (c) if the metric regresses sharply specifically alongside visible component collapse (>90% of responsibility mass on one component across most crops), the mixture family is not refuted, only this round's capacity/init -- the next attempt should add an entropy-floor term on the mixing weights or reduce K rather than abandoning continuous mixture regression; (d) if it regresses sharply with healthy, non-collapsed mixture statistics, compare against exp 41-44's from-scratch-trunk results specifically -- if this lands in their same ~830-1100+ m range, the backbone ban itself (loss of ImageNet features), not the mixture parameterization, is the dominant cost this round, and the next experiment should re-test this exact decode against the best still-legal pretrained alternative rather than iterating the mixture head again.
eli5: For 16 rounds straight, every fix has tried to make one master scoreboard -- ranking up to 1,024 possible neighborhoods against each other -- work a little better, whether that scoreboard was one big table, a filing-cabinet lookup, or a panel of votes. This round throws the scoreboard out completely. Instead, the model gets to say 'I think it's one of these 6 specific spots' (times 4, once per compass direction it double-checks itself from) and picks whichever single guess it's most sure about, the same way a person narrows down a place to a handful of candidates rather than ranking every neighborhood in the city against each other. Because the backbone network powering last round's champion is banned this round, we also had to retire the old sampling trick that leaned on a classroom-style grid of neighborhoods, and replace it with one that works location-by-location instead.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: from-scratch 4-block convolutional trunk (no pretrained weights, no ImageNet, ~300-400k params), 128×128 → 8×8 feature grid

stages.name: Multi-view mixture proposal
stages.detail: the crop's four exact 90° pixel-rotation copies each pass through the same trunk and a shared small head; each view proposes 6 candidate spots (a location guess, a fuzziness radius, and a confidence weight) for itself, and all 4×6=24 candidates are pooled into one shared shortlist — no shared scoreboard ranks all 24 against each other at once, each candidate is graded only against how well its own guess and fuzziness explain the true spot

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: of the 24 candidates, the model commits to the single one it's most confident about and reports its guessed spot — no averaging across candidates, so an unsure blend of guesses never gets reported as a fake in-between location

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: reads the winning candidate's own confidence weight, its own fuzziness radius, and how spread out the 24 candidates' confidence is overall; below a tuned bar it says 'no fix' instead of guessing

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: each training pass still draws a fresh set of places per lighting bucket, weighted so a place that looks like some far-off lookalike place gets picked roughly twice as often — now computed per individual place instead of per shared grid square, since there is no more shared grid

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: the model is graded on how well its full spread of 24 candidate guesses — not just the winning one — explains the true spot, weighted by how confident each candidate claims to be (proper multi-candidate likelihood grading, replacing the old single-scoreboard grading)

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: one shared learning-rate glide for the whole network (previously the pretrained trunk was nudged 10x more gently than the rest; a from-scratch trunk has nothing pretrained to protect, so that split is gone) — same 24 fresh-draw passes and cosine glide-to-zero otherwise

implementation_brief: In model/model.py: (1) Delete _build_pretrained_trunk, PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH, IMAGENET_MEAN/STD, the torchvision import, and GRID_K/_grid_centers/DECODE_BETA (no more grid). Add a from-scratch conv trunk class/function (e.g. 4x [Conv2d(stride=2) + BatchNorm/GroupNorm + ReLU], channels roughly 3->32->64->96->128, 128x128 input -> 8x8 spatial output) with no pretrained loading. (2) Keep the layout_squeeze idea (a 1x1 conv squeezing the trunk's 8x8 feature map to a handful of channels, flattened) concatenated with the GAP descriptor -- this feeds the mixture head, same rationale as exp 10 (GAP alone loses WHERE features sit). (3) Replace loc_logits/dark_logits/gate with one nn.Linear(descriptor_dim, K*4) per-view head, K=6, producing per component: raw_u, raw_v (squash through sigmoid to land in [0,1]), raw_log_sigma (clamp e.g. to [-4, 1] i.e. sigma in ~[0.018, 2.7] normalized units, to prevent numerical blowup/collapse), and mix_logit. (4) In forward(): run the shared trunk+head on the C4-stacked input exactly as _c4_stack already produces (keep _c4_stack unchanged), producing [4N, K, 4] raw outputs per view; reshape to pool the 4 views' K components into one [N, 4*K, ...] set per crop (view axis becomes an extra component axis, not averaged); softmax mix_logit across the pooled 4*K axis to get final per-crop mixing weights; decode u,v as the mean_u/mean_v of the argmax-weight component (torch.argmax + gather, done in a way that stays ONNX-exportable -- test export after writing this, since argmax/gather export can be opset-finicky; if it fails to trace cleanly, use a hard one-hot via a very high-temperature softmax as a differentiable-enough proxy for export purposes, decode must still functionally select one component's mean, not blend). (5) loss_fn: replace the CE+coord_loss body with exact mixture NLL: log-density of the 2D isotropic Gaussian for each of the 4*K components against target_uv, log-sum-exp combine with the mixing weights' log-softmax, negate and mean over the batch; keep the conf BCE term structurally the same shape but recompute pred[:,2] source from the new confidence path (below) instead of the old field-shape conf_head. (6) Confidence: build conf_head input from {winning component's own post-hoc-normalized mixing weight, winning component's own sigma, entropy of the mixing-weight distribution over all 4*K components} (all detached, matching the existing detach discipline) instead of {peak, ent, gap}; keep the same small MLP shape, the same conf_shift registered buffer, and the same sigmoid(z - conf_shift) output -- train.py's calibrate_conf_shift needs NO changes since it only calls model(xb) and reads out[:,2]. (7) export_onnx and estimate_position: unchanged (still a 1x3x128x128 -> [[u,v,conf]] contract) -- just confirm the new forward() traces through torch.onnx.export cleanly given the argmax/gather in decode (see note in step 4). In model/train.py: (1) compute_cell_weights: remove the GRID_K x GRID_K binning (row_splits/np.array_split into k blocks); instead compute the same 4-number (mean R, mean G, mean B, luminance std) descriptor directly per enumerable crop position (or a fine fixed-size raster tile, e.g. matching STRIDE_PX or a small multiple of it, sized so the computation stays cheap and one-time-per-area) via the same reference.tif read, then for each crop find its nearest DISTANT crop/tile in descriptor space (excluding itself and nearby positions within NEIGHBOR_EXCLUDE_R's spatial radius in pixels rather than grid cells) and set weight = 1/(dist+eps), normalized to mean 1.0. (2) In train_area: delete GRID_K import and the crop_cell computation block (lines building crop_cell via meta_u/meta_v * GRID_K); crop_probs is now built directly from the new per-crop weights returned by compute_cell_weights, blended with uniform via the existing CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA exactly as before. (3) Optimizer: replace the trunk_params/head_params 1e-4/1e-3 split with a single param group at 1e-3 (or whatever single LR trains stably for a from-scratch trunk -- reference exp 41-44's from-scratch attempts for a starting LR if their code/logs specify one), same CosineAnnealingLR schedule. (4) info dict's "init" field must say "from-scratch" (not the old pretrained string) and add a line noting K (mixture components per view) and total pooled components for the SQLite log. Preserve exactly: train.py's CLI (--area/--out-dir/--data-dir/--epochs/--max-crops-per-bucket/--seed), model/model.py's ONNX export contract, and the MIN_KEEP_RATE/SCORER_CONF_THRESHOLD calibration mechanism in calibrate_conf_shift (do not touch that function's body). Do not reference mnv3s_features8.pt, mobilenet_v3_small, or import torchvision.models anywhere in model/ -- backbonecheck.py scans the real source (docstrings stripped via ast) and will reject the run if any of those identifiers remain.
